features.title: Three Specialized Tracks
features.description: Urbanisme, Gestion des Villes and Génie Urbain, each with a dedicated curriculum and studio.

features.title: GIS & Digital Tools
features.description: Hands-on training in ArcGIS, QGIS, AutoCAD and BIM from the second year onward.

features.title: Real Urban Projects
features.description: Studios work on live case studies from M'Sila and surrounding wilayas.

features.title: Career-Focused Curriculum
features.description: Programs aligned with the skills employers and public agencies actually look for.

features.title: Active Student Life
features.description: The Urban Pioneers Club runs workshops, field trips and civic-tech projects year-round.

features.title: Open Resource Library
features.description: Lecture notes, GIS files and past exams available to every enrolled student.

testimonials.name: Amel K.
testimonials.quote: The studio projects made me think about M'Sila differently — every plan we drew was for a real street I walk on.

testimonials.name: Yacine B.
testimonials.quote: The governance and smart-city courses prepared me directly for my role at the municipal planning office.

testimonials.name: Sarah M.
testimonials.quote: I came in knowing structural math, and left knowing how a whole neighborhood's infrastructure fits together.

timeline.event: IGTU founded as a specialized institute within Université Mohamed Boudiaf, M'Sila.
Génie Urbain specialty introduced alongside the founding Urbanisme track.
GIS laboratory established, equipping students with ArcGIS and QGIS training.
Gestion des Villes specialty launched to meet growing demand for urban administrators.
Urban Pioneers Club founded by students to extend learning beyond the classroom.
First civic-tech student project, UrbanFix DZ, deployed for public use in M'Sila.

facilities.name: GIS & Cartography Lab
facilities.description: Dedicated computer lab equipped with licensed ArcGIS and QGIS workstations.

facilities.name: Urban Design Studio
facilities.description: Open studio space for model-making, drafting and collaborative design reviews.

facilities.name: Documentation Center
facilities.description: Institute library holding planning texts, theses, and technical reports.

facilities.name: Conference Hall
facilities.description: Venue for seminars, thesis defenses and guest lectures.

faqGeneral.a: IGTU is located within Université Mohamed Boudiaf, M'Sila, Algeria.
Three specialties: Urbanisme, Gestion des Villes, and Génie Urbain, available from the Licence's third year and throughout the Master's program.

faqGeneral.q: How do I apply for a Licence or Master program?
faqGeneral.a: Applications go through the national university orientation platform for Licence, and through the faculty's Master admission process based on your Licence average and specialty compatibility.
Yes, both Licence and Master programs include mandatory internships in their final semesters.

faqGeneral.q: Does IGTU have a student club?
faqGeneral.a: Yes, the Urban Pioneers Club (نادي رواد العمارة) organizes workshops, field trips and civic-tech projects for students.

faqGeneral.q: Can I access course resources online?
faqGeneral.a: Yes, the Resources page hosts lecture notes, TD/TP files, past exams and GIS project files organized by specialty.

contact.address: Institut de Gestion et Techniques Urbaines, Université Mohamed Boudiaf, M'Sila, Algeria

research.title: Urban Resilience & Climate Adaptation
research.description: Applied studies on how M'Sila's neighborhoods can adapt to heat and flood risk.

research.title: GIS-Based Land Monitoring
research.description: Satellite and cadastral data used to track informal growth and land-use change.

research.title: Smart Infrastructure Systems
research.description: Sensor and dashboard prototypes for water, traffic and energy networks.

studentLife.title: Urban Pioneers Club
studentLife.description: Workshops, field trips and civic-tech builds run entirely by students.

studentLife.title: Design Studio Culture
studentLife.description: Open studio critiques where every year's cohort reviews each other's work.

studentLife.title: Field Trips & Site Visits
studentLife.description: Regular visits to active planning sites across M'Sila and neighboring wilayas.
